description: Advanced retrieval augmented generation knowledge items for chunking, query rewriting, reranking, hydration, evaluation, and production RAG reliability.

questions.content: What is Parent-Child Chunking in RAG, and why is it useful?
reference_points: Child chunks are small retrieval units optimized for recall
Parent documents are larger generation units that preserve full context
Retrieval matches fine-grained evidence while generation receives coherent context
It is useful for structured documents with sections, answers, or metadata
A parent_id links each child chunk back to the source document
follow_up_directions: How would you choose child chunk size?
What metadata must each child chunk store?

questions.content: How does structure-aware chunking differ from fixed-size chunking?
reference_points: Fixed-size chunking splits by character or token count
Structure-aware chunking respects semantic fields such as title, section, question, answer, and table
It reduces the chance of cutting a useful unit in half
It keeps metadata aligned with natural document boundaries
It is especially suitable for JSON, Markdown, API docs, and question banks
follow_up_directions: When is fixed-size chunking still acceptable?
How would you chunk a PDF with poor structure?

questions.content: What is contextualized chunking in a RAG system?
reference_points: Each chunk receives a compact header with source context
Context can include document title, category, section path, skill tags, or parent question
This makes isolated chunks more understandable to the embedding model
It improves both vector retrieval and keyword retrieval
The header should be concise to avoid diluting the actual chunk content
follow_up_directions: What context is useful versus noisy?
How would contextualized chunks affect token cost?

questions.content: How would you generate query variants for a RAG index?
reference_points: Create likely user questions or interviewer questions for each document
Use rule-based templates or an offline LLM generation job
Index generated queries as child chunks or auxiliary searchable text
Link generated queries back to the original parent document
Evaluate whether generated queries improve recall without adding noise
follow_up_directions: Would you generate queries online or offline?
How would you avoid low-quality synthetic queries?

questions.content: What is query rewriting in RAG, and when is it useful?
reference_points: Query rewriting transforms the user's original query into retrieval-friendly forms
It can expand abbreviations, add domain terms, or split a broad query into subqueries
It is useful when user wording differs from document wording
Rewriting can be rule-based or LLM-based
Multi-query retrieval can improve recall but needs fusion and deduplication
follow_up_directions: How would you control query drift?
How many rewritten queries are enough?

questions.content: How does hybrid retrieval combine vector search and BM25?
reference_points: Vector search captures semantic similarity
BM25 captures exact keyword and technical term matches
Hybrid retrieval is useful for framework names, APIs, and domain abbreviations
Results can be fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion or weighted score normalization
Hybrid search often improves recall compared with either method alone
follow_up_directions: Why is score normalization difficult across retrievers?
What kinds of queries favor BM25?

questions.content: What is Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and why is it used in RAG retrieval?
reference_points: RRF fuses multiple ranked lists using reciprocal rank positions
It does not require raw scores from different retrievers to be comparable
Items that appear high in multiple lists receive stronger final scores
It works well for fusing vector, BM25, and multi-query results
The k parameter controls how much top ranks dominate
follow_up_directions: How would you tune the RRF k value?
When might weighted fusion be better?

questions.content: What is reranking in RAG, and what are common reranking approaches?
reference_points: Reranking reorders initially retrieved candidates using a more precise scoring method
Rule-based reranking can use metadata, skill overlap, freshness, or business context
Cross-encoder rerankers score query-document pairs with stronger semantic matching
LLM reranking can reason over candidates but is slower and more expensive
Reranking should run on a moderate candidate set rather than the full corpus
follow_up_directions: When would you choose rule-based reranking?
How would you measure reranker improvement?

questions.content: How would you design metadata-aware reranking for an interview question RAG system?
reference_points: Boost questions that overlap with required JD skills
Boost questions related to resume projects or missing skills
Prefer medium and hard questions in professional rounds
Favor system design and AI trend categories in second-round interviews
Penalize overrepresented categories to preserve diversity
follow_up_directions: How would you avoid overfitting reranking rules?
What metadata should be logged for debugging?

questions.content: What is parent hydration after retrieval?
reference_points: Hydration maps retrieved child chunks back to their parent documents
It aggregates scores and evidence from multiple child chunks
It provides the generator with complete and coherent context
It preserves chunk ids for source-level traceability
It prevents the generator from seeing only isolated fragments
follow_up_directions: How would you aggregate scores across child chunks?
What happens if many chunks from one parent dominate retrieval?

questions.content: How would you evaluate retrieval quality in a RAG system?
reference_points: Create golden cases with queries and expected document ids
Use Hit@K to check whether at least one relevant document is retrieved
Use Recall@K to measure coverage of expected references
Use MRR to reward higher rank for the first relevant result
Track metrics for vector-only, hybrid, reranked, and hydrated variants
follow_up_directions: How would you build golden cases efficiently?
What does high Hit@5 but low Recall@5 indicate?

questions.content: What is context precision and context recall in RAG evaluation?
reference_points: Context precision measures how much retrieved context is actually relevant
Context recall measures whether necessary evidence was retrieved
High recall with low precision can overload the generator with noise
High precision with low recall can miss key evidence
These metrics complement answer-level faithfulness and relevance
follow_up_directions: How would you estimate context relevance without labels?
How do retrieval metrics differ from answer metrics?

questions.content: How would you choose top_k in a RAG retrieval pipeline?
reference_points: Small top_k may miss relevant evidence
Large top_k may add noise and increase prompt cost
Use a larger candidate set before reranking and a smaller set after reranking
Tune top_k with retrieval metrics and downstream generation quality
Use diversity control when top results are too similar
follow_up_directions: Why might top_k differ between retrieval and generation?
How would you tune top_k for a small corpus?

questions.content: What are common failure modes in RAG systems?
reference_points: The retriever returns irrelevant documents due to poor query-document alignment
Chunking loses important context or splits related information
The generator ignores retrieved evidence
The system retrieves stale or duplicate information
Evaluation focuses only on final answers and misses retrieval failures
follow_up_directions: How would you debug a bad answer?
How would you distinguish retrieval failure from generation failure?

questions.content: How would you design source tracking in a parent-child RAG system?
reference_points: Track parent document ids for user-facing citations
Track child chunk ids for retrieval debugging
Store chunk type and matched chunk text for explainability
Validate that generated source ids come from the retrieved set
Use source tracking in offline evaluation and manual review
follow_up_directions: Should users see chunk ids or parent ids?
How would you handle generated content with no source?

questions.content: How can diversity control improve RAG retrieval results?
reference_points: It prevents one category or document from dominating the final context
It improves coverage across multiple skills or topics
It is useful after multi-query retrieval where duplicate themes can appear
It can cap results per category, difficulty, source, or parent id
It should not remove highly relevant evidence too aggressively
follow_up_directions: How would you choose diversity caps?
When can diversity hurt answer quality?

questions.content: What is HyDE in RAG, and when would you use it?
reference_points: HyDE generates a hypothetical answer or document from the query
The hypothetical text is embedded and used for retrieval
It can help when the original query is short or poorly aligned with documents
It adds latency and model cost because it needs a generation step
It should be evaluated carefully because generated text can introduce drift
follow_up_directions: Why might HyDE hurt precision?
Would you use HyDE in an interview question planner?

questions.content: What is corrective RAG, and what problem does it try to solve?
reference_points: Corrective RAG detects when retrieved context is insufficient or irrelevant
It can trigger query rewriting, web search, broader retrieval, or fallback behavior
It is useful when retrieval quality varies across topics
It requires a confidence or relevance judgment step
It is more complex than basic RAG and should be justified by failure cases
follow_up_directions: How would you detect low-quality retrieval?
What fallback would you choose for a small local question bank?

questions.content: How would you reduce hallucination in a RAG-based answer generation system?
reference_points: Retrieve relevant and sufficient context before generation
Instruct the model to use only provided sources when factual grounding is required
Track citations or source ids for generated claims
Use answer validation or faithfulness checks for high-risk outputs
Improve chunk quality, query rewriting, and reranking when context is weak
follow_up_directions: Can RAG eliminate hallucination completely?
How would you test faithfulness?

questions.content: How would you migrate from a small RAG corpus to a larger production knowledge base?
reference_points: Introduce stable document ids and chunk ids before scaling
Separate offline indexing from online retrieval services
Track index versions and embedding model versions
Add incremental indexing and deletion support
Use retrieval evaluation to catch regressions after corpus updates
follow_up_directions: How would you handle changed documents?
How would you compare two index versions?

questions.content: How would you handle metadata filters in RAG retrieval?
reference_points: Metadata filters constrain retrieval to categories, difficulties, users, or document types
Filters improve precision when the user intent is clear
Overly strict filters can reduce recall
BM25 and vector branches should apply compatible filters in hybrid retrieval
Filter usage should be logged for debugging poor retrieval
follow_up_directions: Would you filter before or after vector search?
How would filters interact with reranking?

questions.content: What is the role of embeddings in RAG, and how would you choose an embedding model?
reference_points: Embeddings map text into vectors for semantic similarity search
The model should fit language, domain, latency, cost, and dimension requirements
Embedding changes require reindexing or index versioning
Evaluate embedding models with retrieval golden cases
Domain-specific terms may still benefit from BM25 even with good embeddings
follow_up_directions: Why must you reindex after changing embedding models?
How would you compare two embedding models?

questions.content: How would you design incremental indexing for a RAG system?
reference_points: Compute stable ids for documents and chunks
Detect added, changed, and deleted source records
Only re-embed changed chunks to save cost
Upsert changed chunks and remove obsolete chunks
Record index version and source file metadata
follow_up_directions: How would you detect content changes?
How would you rollback a bad index update?

questions.content: How would you debug a RAG system that returns irrelevant interview questions?
reference_points: Log rewritten queries, top-k candidates, scores, and source metadata
Check whether chunking lost useful context
Compare vector-only, BM25, hybrid, and reranked results
Inspect whether generated query chunks are too broad
Add failed examples to the retrieval golden dataset
follow_up_directions: What would you inspect first?
How would you know if the issue is query rewriting or chunking?

questions.content: How would you decide whether an advanced RAG technique is worth adding?
reference_points: Start from observed failure cases rather than adding techniques by name
Estimate complexity, latency, cost, and maintainability
Measure retrieval and downstream generation quality before and after the change
Prefer simple metadata or chunking improvements when they solve the problem
Document why a technique was chosen and what alternatives were rejected
follow_up_directions: Why not always use GraphRAG or Self-RAG?
How would you present this tradeoff in a project interview?
